name: Assam Spicy Shrimp
recipeIngredient: 3 ounces Young ginger root, finely grated or slivered
recipeInstructions:
1. Break tamarind apart and place in a small saucepan with water. Simmer, covered, for about 10 minutes or until the pulp separates from the seeds. Press through a sieve to extract all the liquid. Discard the seeds.
2. Rinse shrimp thoroughly and pat dry. Marinate for 1 hour with 1 tablespoon of this tamarind liquid, oyster sauce, and hot chili sauce.
3. Meanwhile, cut young ginger into toothpick-sized slivers. Peel and slice garlic.
4. Place blachan in a non-stick pan and heat gently for 3 to 4 minutes to develop the flavors.
5. Peel and slice red onions into wedges.
6. Combine oyster sauce, soy sauce, chili sauce, and blachan; stir into remaining tamarind liquid.
7. Heat oil in a large wok over high heat until it smokes slightly.
8. Add ginger and garlic, stirring frequently, until golden brown, about 3 to 4 minutes.
9. Add shrimp in batches and cook until pink and opaque, about 3 minutes per batch.
10. Pour the sauce mixture over the shrimp and continue cooking at a brisk boil until aromatic and thickened, about 8 minutes.
11. Mix in sugar and onion wedges, tasting and adjusting seasoning as needed for sweetness and spiciness.
Makes plenty for 6 as a main dish with rice, or enough for 8 with additional dishes.
